# Anti-Patterns — What Not to Do

**Anti-Pattern 1: Re-Reading. Most common and least effective study method.**

**Anti-Pattern 2: Highlighting. Gives false sense of learning. Does not build memory.**

**Case: The All-Nighter. Pulling an all-nighter destroys memory consolidation.**

**Case: The Multitasking. The brain cannot multitask. You just switch poorly.**

**Case: The Passivity. Watching without doing is not learning.**

**Case: The Cramming. Cramming works for short-term but not long-term retention.**
---

**Case: The Distraction. Phone notifications destroy focus. Put your phone away.**
**Case: The Procrastination Loop. Procrastination creates guilt, which creates more procrastination.**
**Case: The Illusion of Competence. Feeling like you know something when you have not tested yourself.**
**Case: The Overlearning. Practicing what you already know while neglecting what you do not.**
**Case: The Comparison. Comparing yourself to others is a distraction. Focus on your own progress.**
**Case: The Summative. Only testing at the end of a course, not throughout. Formative assessment is better.**
**Case: The Lecture. Passive listening is one of the least effective ways to learn.**
**Case: The Comfort Zone. Staying in your comfort zone prevents growth.**
**Case: The Overconfidence. Assuming you know something without testing yourself.**
